The plan of the specific sub-structure 1: {'name': 'Single-piece Tower-Bridge Span', 'description': 'A one-piece bridge incorporating: two open-frame portal towers at y≈±5 with legs at x=±2; a suspended deck along y from about −6 to +6 at z≈5; multiple rope winch hangers from tower tops to deck; and decorative elements (powered wheels as “clock faces,” torches as lanterns, and a water cannon finial). Temporary deck-level arms used for tower erection are removed after rope suspension to create the characteristic hollowed arches and vertical layering.', 'components': '- Base: 1 default 1×1×1 stone starting block (mandatory).
        - Small Wooden Block (approx. 78–80 final count, kept < 100 total including connectors):
          - Central kingpost and deck spine along y (≈12).
          - Tower lateral arm stubs to x=±2 at y=±5 for each side, used in erection then partially removed.
          - Four tower legs (two per tower): each leg extends up ≈3 blocks above deck and down ≈5 blocks below deck.
          - Minimal ornamental caps and small ledges at tower tops.
        - Winch (8 total):
          - 4 on the north tower, 4 on the south tower (acting as rope suspenders to the deck).
        - Brace (6 total):
          - 2 top cross-ties (one per tower, linking the two legs at high level).
          - 2 low cross-ties (one per tower, linking the two legs near the base).
          - 2 diagonal ties from deck to lower tower legs (one per tower) to preserve lateral unity after scaffold removal.
        - Powered Wheel (2 total):
          - Decorative “clock-face” discs, one centered on each tower’s outer face.
        - Torch (4 total):
          - Lanterns on tower exteriors, oriented so the 0.3-radius flame sphere is outside wood surfaces.
        - Water Cannon (1 total):
          - Decorative finial mounted high, control unbound, not fired.', 'assembly': '1) Establish the deck elevation and spine
           - From the default base stone block, stack 5 Small Wooden Blocks vertically to create a central kingpost (sets the working deck elevation at z≈5).
           - From the kingpost’s top, extend a straight Small Wooden Block chain along the y-axis to form a deck spine ≈12 blocks long (from about y=−6 to y=+6 at x=0, z≈5). This assures a span longer than the 10‑unit gap.

        2) Create tower arm stubs at y≈±5
           - At the deck blocks located at y=+5 and y=−5 (x=0, z≈5), attach two-block lateral arms in both +x and −x directions:
             • At each y=±5 position, place a block on the +x face (x=+1), then another on the +x face of that block (x=+2).
             • Repeat to the −x side (x=−1 and x=−2).
           - These 4 arms (two at each tower location) temporarily couple the upcoming tower legs to the deck for erection.

        3) Build the tower legs (two towers, each with two legs at x=±2, y=±5)
           - For each of the four arm tips (x=+2 and x=−2 at y=+5, and x=+2 and x=−2 at y=−5), create vertical leg columns:
             • Upward: stack 3 Small Wooden Blocks (to about z≈8).
             • Downward: stack 5 Small Wooden Blocks (to about z≈0) so the feet will contact the banks when the structure settles.
           - This forms two portal frames: a north tower at y=+5 and a south tower at y=−5.

        4) Cross-tie the tower legs with volumeless Braces
           - High-level cap: add 1 Brace per tower between the inner faces of the two top leg blocks (z≈8–9), creating a rigid top tie that also serves as a rope anchor “saddle.”
           - Low-level tie: add 1 Brace per tower between the inner faces of the two lower leg blocks near z≈1–2 (below deck), stabilizing the base and reinforcing the open lower arch look.

        5) Suspend the deck with Winches (ropes)
           - North tower (y=+5):
             • From the −y faces of both top leg blocks (x=−2 and x=+2, z≈8), run 4 Winches down to the top faces of deck blocks at y=+4 and y=+2 (two anchors per y).
           - South tower (y=−5):
             • From the +y faces of both top leg blocks (x=−2 and x=+2, z≈8), run 4 Winches down to the top faces of deck blocks at y=−4 and y=−2 (two anchors per y).
           - This creates a set of symmetrical rope hangers between towers and deck, allowing a gentle “sagged” suspended appearance and ensuring the deck’s vertical support is via ropes.

        6) Preserve overall unity while freeing the deck (scaffold removal and lateral ties)
           - Before removal: add 1 Brace per tower diagonally from the deck block at y=±5 (use a side face) to a lower leg block at the same tower (e.g., to the inner face of the leg near z≈2). These braces provide lateral linkage so the whole build remains a single sub-structure after removing arm blocks.
           - Remove the inner deck-level scaffold blocks at x=±1 at y=±5 (the intermediate arm blocks that directly touch the deck). Leave the outermost arm tip blocks at x=±2 only if still needed to access/retain tower alignment during build; ideally remove those as well if the diagonal brace is in place and the tower is fully stable. The goal is to leave the deck visually and structurally suspended by the winch ropes while keeping a minimal lateral brace link to satisfy single-piece assembly and provide horizontal stability.

        7) Add ornamental elements
           - Powered Wheels (2): Attach one to each tower’s outer vertical face at about z≈7 (east face on the east leg and west face on the west leg), acting as rotating “clock gears.” Do not press their control keys so they remain stopped/braked unless a visual spin is desired during display.
           - Torches (4): Mount as lanterns on the outer faces of the legs (e.g., at z≈6–7), oriented outward so the flame’s 0.3‑unit heating sphere is fully outside the wood structure. Avoid placing near Winch ropes. Example: attach a torch to the east face of the east leg and point it further east; attach a torch to the west face of the west leg and point it further west on both towers.
           - Water Cannon (1): Place centrally above the deck around y≈0, x≈0, z≈7 as a decorative finial pointing upward or outward. Leave controls unbound so it never fires (prevents recoil and inadvertent torch extinguishing).', 'motion_control': '- Do not bind or press Water Cannon controls; it must remain idle.
        - Do not bind or press Powered Wheel controls unless a static visual spin is desired briefly; otherwise keep them braked.
        - Winches: Once connected, leave at default length so the deck hangs under gravity. No active control needed during evaluation.
        - The structure is static; it will be placed above the gap and allowed to settle with the tower feet contacting the banks. The deck remains ≥5 units above the gap bottom by design (deck elevation set at z≈5 relative to tower foot pads).', 'function': '- The two portal towers supply vertical elevation and rope attachment points; their lower legs act as feet to rest on the banks.
        - The deck spans the 10‑unit north–south gap with about 12 units overall length to ensure overlap and seating.
        - Winches (ropes) provide primary vertical support, suspending the deck from the tower tops in the spirit of London Tower Bridge.
        - Braces lock the towers internally and provide minimal lateral ties to the deck after scaffold removal, preserving a single-piece assembly and resisting sway.
        - Decorative wheels, torches, and the water cannon enhance architectural richness and block diversity without altering stability.', 'design_requirements': '- Orientation: The bridge spans along the north–south (y) axis; place so towers align near y≈±5.
        - Dimensions: Deck elevation ≈ z=5; tower tops ≈ z=8–9; tower feet down to ≈ z=0. Deck length ≈ 12 units (y from −6 to +6), ensuring full coverage of the 10‑unit gap.
        - Suspension: Ensure all 8 winches are attached from tower-top inner faces to deck top faces at y=±4 and ±2. After attaching, remove the intermediate arm blocks at x=±1 at y=±5 to leave the deck primarily suspended by ropes.
        - Bracing: Keep 6 Braces total—two top cross-ties (one per tower), two low cross-ties (one per tower), and two diagonal deck-to-tower ties (one per tower) to maintain single-piece integrity and lateral stability.
        - Fire safety: Orient torches outward so the 0.3‑unit flame sphere does not intersect any wood, rope, or cannon body. Do not fire the Water Cannon.
        - Controls: Leave Water Cannon and Wheels unbound/inactive during evaluation.
        - Block count: Keep total under 100 for this single sub-structure; target ≈99 or fewer after scaffold removal (≈78–80 Small Wooden Blocks, 8 Winches, 6 Braces, 2 Powered Wheels, 4 Torches, 1 Water Cannon, plus the default base stone).
        - Positions may be micro‑adjusted in later stages to resolve conflicts based on actual build execution.'}

Sub-structure: Single-piece Tower-Bridge Span
Global axes: +x east, +y north, +z up. A default 1×1×1 base block exists at [0,0,0]; all first attachments reference its faces as needed. All blocks are attached to existing block faces; connectors link faces of two existing blocks. Final target span runs along the y-axis from ≈y=−6 to y=+6 with deck at z≈5, towers at y≈±5, legs at x=±2.

Keep control unbound/inactive so it remains braked.

Water cannon finial (leave idle)
85 - Water Cannon - Decorative finial centered above deck (y≈0), pointing up - top of Block 78 - Function: Axis vertical; recoil intentionally unused. Leave controls unbound; do not fire. Inlet near z≈5.625, outlet near z≈7.375; body well clear of torches’ steam/fire and ropes.

Structural braces (6 total; volumeless connectors linking two faces)
86 - Brace - North tower top cross-tie - relative position: between Block 34 west face and Block 44 east face - Note: High-level rigid tie at z≈8 links the two north tower legs internally.
87 - Brace - South tower top cross-tie - relative position: between Block 54 west face and Block 64 east face - Note: High-level rigid tie at z≈8 links the two south tower legs.
88 - Brace - North tower low cross-tie - relative position: between Block 37 west face and Block 47 east face - Note: Low-level tie near z≈2 stabilizes base.
89 - Brace - South tower low cross-tie - relative position: between Block 57 west face and Block 67 east face - Note: Low-level tie near z≈2 stabilizes base.
90 - Brace - North diagonal deck-to-leg tie - relative position: between Block 10 east face and Block 37 west face - Note: Install after removing temp arm Block 30; preserves single-piece unity and lateral stability.
91 - Brace - South diagonal deck-to-leg tie - relative position: between Block 16 west face and Block 67 east face - Note: Install after removing temp arm Block 60; preserves single-piece unity and lateral stability.

Suspension winches (8 total; rope hangers from tower tops to deck top faces)
92 - Winch - North tower hanger 1 - relative position: from Block 34 (−y face) to Block 9 (top face) - Note: Suspends deck at y=+4 from north-east top.
93 - Winch - North tower hanger 2 - relative position: from Block 44 (−y face) to Block 9 (top face) - Note: Suspends deck at y=+4 from north-west top.
94 - Winch - North tower hanger 3 - relative position: from Block 34 (−y face) to Block 7 (top face) - Note: Suspends deck at y=+2 from north-east top.
95 - Winch - North tower hanger 4 - relative position: from Block 44 (−y face) to Block 7 (top face) - Note: Suspends deck at y=+2 from north-west top.
96 - Winch - South tower hanger 1 - relative position: from Block 54 (+y face) to Block 15 (top face) - Note: Suspends deck at y=−4 from south-east top.
97 - Winch - South tower hanger 2 - relative position: from Block 64 (+y face) to Block 15 (top face) - Note: Suspends deck at y=−4 from south-west top.
98 - Winch - South tower hanger 3 - relative position: from Block 54 (+y face) to Block 13 (top face) - Note: Suspends deck at y=−2 from south-east top.
99 - Winch - South tower hanger 4 - relative position: from Block 64 (+y face) to Block 13 (top face) - Note: Suspends deck at y=−2 from south-west top.

Scaffold removal (after all winches 92–99 and diagonal braces 90–91 are installed)
- Remove Block 30 (temp arm x=+1 at y=+5), Block 40 (temp arm x=−1 at y=+5), Block 50 (temp arm x=+1 at y=−5), and Block 60 (temp arm x=−1 at y=−5). This frees the deck under each tower while maintaining a single-piece assembly via ropes and diagonal braces.

Functional behavior and controls
- Powered Wheels (Blocks 79, 80): Decorative only; leave controls unbound/off so they remain braked. If enabled, each vertical disc would produce rolling along the north–south axis parallel to its attached face.
- Torches (Blocks 81–84): Oriented upward/outward; each flame sphere (radius 0.3) is centered 1 unit outward and 1 unit above its mounting point, clear of wood/rope/wheel surfaces. Avoids accidental ignition.
- Water Cannon (Block 85): Decorative finial, pointing upward. Leave control unbound; do not fire. No recoil will be produced.
- Winches (Blocks 92–99): Leave at default length for a gentle catenary; no active control required.

Position adjustment notes
- Raised finial: Added small wooden pedestal (Block 78) so the Water Cannon center sits ≈z=6.5 and outlet ≈z=7.375, matching the “around z≈7” design intent.
- Wheel clearance: Outward ledges were attached to cap blocks at z=9 (Blocks 74–77) rather than to z=8 leg faces to ensure the 2‑unit-diameter wheel discs at z≈7 have full clearance (no coplanar overlap).
- Torch clearance: Torches placed one block below wheels (z≈6). With flame sphere center ~1 unit outward and upward from the mounting face and sphere radius 0.3, minimum clearance to the wheel body is ≥0.2 units in x, preventing heating/ignition of the wheels.

Final block count (after scaffold removal)
- Small Wooden Blocks: 78
- Braces: 6
- Winches: 8
- Powered Wheels: 2
- Torches: 4
- Water Cannon: 1
Total: 99 (under 100), plus the default base.

Assembly rationale
- Deck spine spans y≈−6..+6 at z≈5 to clear a 10‑unit gap.
- Two portal towers at y=±5 with legs at x=±2 rise to z≈8–9 and drop to z≈0, with internal braces for rigidity.
- Eight winch hangers suspend the deck from tower tops (y=±5 → deck y=±4, ±2).
- After installing winches and diagonal braces, temporary deck-level arm blocks at x=±1 are removed to reveal the hollowed arches while retaining lateral ties for single-piece integrity.

Reviewer analysis for “Single-piece Tower-Bridge Span”

Conventions
- Coordinate frame: +x east, +y north, +z up. Base block (1×1×1) center at [0,0,0]. Small Wooden Blocks (SWB) are 1×1×1 cubes.
- When a SWB is attached to a face of another SWB, centers differ by exactly 1 along that face-normal axis. Minimum non-overlap separation along that axis is 1 (0.5 + 0.5), so face-to-face touching at 1 unit is acceptable.
- Powered Wheel shape: [2,2,0.5]; axis perpendicular to attached face; we assume the wheel center lies 0.25 outward from the face (thickness/2) unless otherwise specified. Disc radius = 1 in the wheel plane. We will flag where this assumption matters.
- Torch: using the given example, torch body center is 0.5 outboard from the mounting face along the face normal. If the torch is oriented “up,” the heating sphere center is 1 unit above the torch body center (i.e., body + [0,0,1]) and has radius 0.3.
- Water Cannon: center is 0.5 away from the mounting face along the orientation direction (as per the official example). Length along axis 1.75; endpoints are ±0.875 from center along the axis.

Step-by-step structural placement and collision checks
Note: For each block, we report its center coordinates and check potential-overlap neighbors (typically the block it attaches to and any blocks whose AABBs could touch). Face-adjacent SWBs have center-to-center distance = 1 along the attach axis and 0 along the other two axes; this is the minimum allowed, i.e., “just touching faces.” We flag any deviation.

Conventions and geometry assumptions
- Axes: +x east, +y north, +z up. Default base block center at [0,0,0].
- Small Wooden Block (SWB) size 1×1×1; face-to-face attachment offsets the center by exactly 1 unit along the face normal. Minimum non-overlap separation along any attach axis is 1 (0.5 + 0.5); face adjacency at distance 1 is OK.
- Powered Wheel shape: [2,2,0.5]. When attached to a vertical face, axis is normal to that face. We take the wheel center 0.25 m outboard of the face (thickness/2), so the x-span from the face is [0, 0.5] and the wheel radius in the face plane (y–z) is 1.
- Torch: Body center is 0.5 outboard from the mounting face along face normal. The flame sphere center is “body center + orientation vector” (length 1) in the given flame direction. Sphere radius r=0.3.
- Water Cannon: When attached to a top face, center is 0.5 above that face (per the official example). Length along its axis is 1.75; the ends are ±0.875 from its center along the axis.
